title: spinulum annotinum subsp. alpestre
intro: Spinulum annotinum subsp. alpestre, commonly known as Mountain Hare's Ear, is a type of lichen that belongs to the family Parmeliaceae. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antifungal and antibacterial compounds, which have been used to treat skin infections and wounds. The health benefits of Mountain Hare's Ear include its ability to aid in wound healing, reduce inflammation, and exhibit antimicrobial activity. The herbal preparations of this plant typically involve drying and grinding it into a powder, which can be used in topical applications or teas to harness its medicinal properties.
